messages.content:
At what point does the selection process start?
A.
when an employer starts screening candidates that have applied for the job
B.
when a job becomes vacant
C.
at the same time as recruitment
D.
when an existing employee provides their formal resignation letter
A. when an employer starts screening candidates that have applied for the job

What would be the focus of selection for a company strategically planning an international expansion?
A.
language skills
B.
cultural sensitivity training
C.
extroversion
D.
gender equity
B. cultural sensitivity training

What is the selection ratio?
A.
number of applicants interviewed ÷ total number of applicants
B.
number of applicants hired ÷ total number of applicants
C.
number of applicants interviewed ÷ number of applicants hired
D.
total number of applicants ÷ number of applicants hired
A. number of applicants interviewed ÷ total number of applicants

hat could a large selection ratio indicate?
A.
Candidates have to be less competitive to succeed.
B.
There is an oversupply of labour available.
C.
The job ad is too specific.
D.
Your employer brand might be poor.
B. There is an oversupply of labour available.

Which of the following is not an example of managing the selection process in a legally defensible way?
A.
Ask questions about compensation history and childcare arrangements indirectly to determine possible accommodations.
B.
Secure written authorization for reference checking from prospective employees.
C.
Keep records about the applicant during each stage of the selection process.
D.
Reject applicants who make false statements on their application forms or résumés.
A. Ask questions about compensation history and childcare arrangements indirectly to determine possible accommodations.

According to research, which of the following tests is the strongest predictor of job success?
A.
general mental ability test
B.
aptitude test
C.
personality test
D.
interest inventories
A. general mental ability test

Which of the following is true about personality tests?
A.
The Minnesota Multiphasic Personality Inventory (MMPI) is believed to be the most widely used personality inventory in the world.
B.
Research studies confirm that personality tests do not help companies hire more effective workers.
C.
Personality tests can be used in career planning, since people generally do better in jobs involving activities in which they have an interest.
D.
Faking is a threat to the validity of personality inventory in the assessment process.
D. Faking is a threat to the validity of personality inventory in the assessment process.

What is differential validity?
A.
the extent that a selection instrument, such as a test, adequately samples the knowledge and skills needed to perform the job
B.
the extent that a selection tool predicts or significantly correlates with important elements of work behaviour
C.
the degree that selection procedures yield comparable data over time
D.
confirmation that a selection tool accurately predicts the performance of all possible employee subgroups
D. confirmation that a selection tool accurately predicts the performance of all possible employee subgroups

When a job candidate is asked to perform key tasks associated with the job that they are applying to, this is known as ___________________.
A.
a personality test
B.
interest inventories
C.
work sampling
D.
grade-point average
C. work sampling

What is the major difference between intelligence quotient (IQ) and emotional intelligence (EQ)?
A.
IQ relates to general intellectual abilities; EQ refers only to a person’s likelihood of becoming emotional.
B.
IQ measures intellectual abilities and EQ measures a person’s ability to understand emotional cues from others.
C.
IQ and EQ are two ways of measuring the same thing.
D.
IQ is a more complete measure of capability than EQ.
B. IQ measures intellectual abilities and EQ measures a person’s ability to understand emotional cues from others.

In which evaluation technique are candidates presented with hypothetical circumstances representative of the job for which they are applying (often on video) and evaluated on their responses?
A.
micro-assessments
B.
interest inventories
C.
work sampling
D.
situational interview
D. situational interview

Which of the following is not a common interviewing mistake?
A.
using situational and behavioural questions
B.
negative emphasis and halo effect
C.
similar-to-me bias and leading
D.
poor planning and snap judgments
A. using situational and behavioural questions

Which of the following is the major benefit of a semi-structured interview compared to a structured interview?
A.
The interview is more conversational.
B.
It is quicker to conduct than the structured interview.
C.
It relies less on knowledge of the job.
D.
The same questions can be used for a variety of jobs.
A. The interview is more conversational.

How are the majority of interviews conducted?
A.
sequentially and with a panel of interviewers
B.
simultaneously and with a panel of interviewers
C.
simultaneously and one on one
D.
sequentially and one on one
D. sequentially and one on one

Jennifer is frustrated. An important job opening that she advertised has received only four responses and only one person meets the minimum qualifications and only marginally. What is likely the most appropriate course of action for Jennifer?
A.
Hire the best candidate of the four who applied.
B.
Repost or readvertise the job, perhaps through different media.
C.
Change the job requirements.
D.
Leave the position open and try again in six months.
B. Repost or readvertise the job, perhaps through different media.

Confirmation that the selection tool accurately predicts the performance of all possible employee subgroups, including white males, women, visible minorities, persons with disabilities, and Indigenous Peoples, is called _______________________.
A.
construct validity
B.
differential validity
C.
criterion-related validity
D.
content validity
B. differential validity

If George has decided to measure the temperature of his coffee using a ruler, which of the following would be true?
A.
George would be using an instrument giving a valid measure, but one that is not reliable.
B.
George would be getting a measure that is neither reliable nor valid.
C.
George would be getting a reliable and valid measure.
D.
George would be getting a reliable measure, but one that is not valid.
D. George would be getting a reliable measure, but one that is not valid.

Bob, Stella, and Amir interviewed two candidates for a position with their organization, Tradco Industries, but at the end of the interviews, Bob, Stella, and Amir had reached vastly different conclusions regarding the candidates. What would be true in this situation?
A.
Bob, Stella, and Amir likely do not know how to interview, as interviews are generally very valid.
B.
This interview is a reliable and valid measure.
C.
The interview is definitely valid, but not reliable.
D.
This interview is not a reliable measure, but it might be valid.
D. This interview is not a reliable measure, but it might be valid.

You are responsible for screening applicants for several vacancies in your company’s sales department. In addition to the usual selection criteria, the hiring manager has asked you to identify outgoing, people-oriented candidates. Which screening tool would be most appropriate?
A.
Myers-Briggs Type Indicator
B.
Crawford Small Parts Dexterity Test
C.
General Aptitude Test Battery
D.
Stanford-Binet Test
A. Myers-Briggs Type Indicator

A strategy used to provide applicants with information—both positive and negative—about the job demands, the organization’s expectations, and the work environment is known as a(n) _______________.
A.
NOC match
B.
realistic job preview
C.
selection criteria
D.
reference check
B. realistic job preview

In an interview for a customer service position with a cellular phone company, Mike was asked a series of questions such as “Tell me about a time when you had a conflict with a co-worker. What happened and how was it resolved?” What type of interview question is Mike experiencing?
A.
assumptive
B.
behavioural
C.
hypothetical
D.
situational
B. behavioural

Elisha has just finished interviewing Jennifer for the position of help desk associate for a computer company. Elisha was very impressed when she first met Jennifer. With Jennifer’s firm handshake and confident demeanour, Elisha is sure that she will perform extremely well in this position. Which of the following most correctly describes what is happening in this situation?
A.
Elisha is experiencing contrast error.
B.
Elisha is experiencing similar-to-me bias.
C.
Elisha is telegraphing.
D.
Elisha is experiencing the halo effect.
D. Elisha is experiencing the halo effect.

Applicants for the job of air traffic controller must be able to visualize objects moving in three dimensions and write a test to determine if they have this ability. What category would this test fall into?
A.
emotional intelligence test
B.
personality test
C.
aptitude test
D.
interest inventory
C. aptitude test

ason was in a hurry to fill his open sales​ position, so he allowed his new employee to start before the background check was complete. The new employee was involved in an incident of workplace theft, and it was later discovered this was not the first time. Jason could be held liable for which of the​ following?
A.
unforeseeable circumstances
B.
improper hiring
C.
negligent hiring
D.
negligent supervision
C. negligent hiring
